title: China and Latin America Archives - Americas Quarterly
gist: An Americas Quarterly archive index tracking China's evolving Latin America strategy—shifting from big loans and infrastructure toward targeted engagement in green tech and security cooperation—amid U.S.-China competition and a mixed regional realignment even as the hemisphere turns rightward.

insights.claim: Beijing is described as shifting from an era of large loans and infrastructure initiatives toward more targeted, strategic engagement in Latin America.
insights.speaker: Americas Quarterly
insights.so_what: China's LatAm exposure is moving from broad capex lending to selective strategic sectors—changes which local assets and firms benefit.
insights.locator: China’s New Playbook for Latin America

insights.claim: China's green-tech investment in Latin America is described as gaining traction and set to intensify even as the hemisphere turns rightward politically.
insights.speaker: Americas Quarterly
insights.so_what: Green-tech (EVs, renewables) is the leading edge of Chinese capital in LatAm—and rightward politics may not slow it, a non-obvious durability signal.
insights.locator: China’s Green-Tech Push in Latin America

insights.claim: Beijing's regional approach is framed as a five-pillar plan marking a new era in China-CELAC relations.
insights.speaker: Americas Quarterly
insights.so_what: Signals an institutionalized, multi-vector China strategy toward the region rather than ad hoc deals.
insights.locator: How China Is Seeking Dominance in Latin America

themes.theme: Security cooperation as a new vector

insights.claim: Beijing is described as finding success by offering security cooperation to Latin America that is very different from that of the U.S.
insights.speaker: Americas Quarterly
insights.so_what: China expanding beyond trade into security ties raises the strategic stakes of the U.S.-China contest in the hemisphere.
insights.locator: China’s Quiet Security Push in Latin America

insights.claim: Brazil's relationship with China is described as gaining momentum, with U.S. policies actively driving Brazil to seek closer collaboration with Beijing.
insights.speaker: Americas Quarterly
insights.so_what: U.S. policy is portrayed as a push factor accelerating Brazil-China ties—counterproductive to Washington's own regional aims.
insights.locator: Brazil Deepens Its Bond With China

insights.claim: Ecuador's President Noboa is described as pursuing a pragmatic strategy with Beijing without unduly alienating Washington.
insights.speaker: Americas Quarterly
insights.so_what: Illustrates the hedging model smaller LatAm states use—neither bloc captures them cleanly.
insights.locator: Noboa’s Balancing Act Between China and the U.S.

insights.claim: Colombia's pivot toward China is described as raising U.S. concerns, with room for Bogotá to pursue a more strategic foreign policy.
insights.speaker: Americas Quarterly
insights.so_what: Colombia, a traditional U.S. ally, tilting toward China would be a notable realignment signal.
insights.locator: Colombia’s China Pivot Raises U.S. Concerns

insights.claim: Panama is cited as realigning with Washington as an early Trump win, but convincing other Latin American states to leave Beijing's orbit is framed as more challenging.
insights.speaker: Americas Quarterly
insights.so_what: China ties are described as sticky and not easily severed—tempering expectations of a broad U.S.-led rollback.
insights.locator: Latin America’s China Ties Won’t Be Easily Severed

insights.claim: The U.S. and China are described as sparring for influence over the Paraguay-Paraná river system, where a surge of projects is attracting geopolitical competition while boosting regional growth.
insights.speaker: Americas Quarterly
insights.so_what: Waterway logistics infrastructure is an under-covered arena of U.S.-China competition tied to South American commodity exports.
insights.locator: U.S. and China Spar for Influence on the Paraguay-Paraná

insights.claim: Peru's Chancay port is highlighted as a focal point of the China-U.S. battle in Latin America, warranting a big-picture read of what it means for the region.
insights.speaker: Americas Quarterly
insights.so_what: Chancay is emblematic of China's port-and-logistics footprint reshaping Pacific trade routes from South America.
